name: Phylum Software Supply Chain API
what_it_does: Software supply chain security platform with REST API — analyzing open-source packages for malware, vulnerabilities, license issues, author risk, and engineering quality using ML-based risk scoring across npm, PyPI, Maven, RubyGems, and other ecosystems in real-time.
use_cases: Scanning package.json/requirements.txt for malicious packages before installation
Blocking malicious dependencies in CI/CD pipelines via API policy enforcement
Querying package risk scores across multiple ecosystems for SBOM enrichment
Monitoring package ecosystem for newly detected malware via webhook alerts
Evaluating open-source package quality and author trustworthiness before adoption
not_for: SAST for custom code (focus is open-source package analysis)
Runtime security monitoring of deployed applications
Basic CVE-only scanning (Phylum goes beyond CVEs to malware detection)
best_when: A security team wants real-time malware detection in package registries and supply chain risk analysis beyond CVE databases, especially for detecting novel attacks.
avoid_when: You need only standard CVE scanning, SAST for proprietary code, or a more established platform with enterprise support like Snyk or Mend.

auth.notes: OAuth2 for application integration. Bearer token auth. Phylum CLI handles authentication flow. API keys for CI/CD integration. OpenAPI spec available. Webhook for package risk score changes.

pricing.notes: Free tier for individual developers. Per-developer pricing for teams. US-based startup (Colorado). Founded by cybersecurity veterans. ML-based malware detection differentiates from CVE-only tools. Packages analyzed daily for emerging threats. Strong GitHub and GitLab CI integration.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API with OpenAPI spec for packages, projects, groups, and policy. OAuth2 for integrations. Webhook for risk alerts. Phylum CLI wraps API for common workflows. ML risk scores returned for packages. Package analysis may take minutes for new/unknown packages.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Package analysis requests idempotent for known packages (cached results). New package analysis creates async job. Project management idempotent by ID. Policy evaluation always fresh.
known_agent_gotchas: Package analysis is async for unknown packages — poll for completion
Risk scores cover 5 dimensions: vulnerability, malicious code, author risk, engineering quality, license
Phylum CLI preferred for most workflows — REST API for programmatic integration
Ecosystem support: npm, PyPI, Maven, RubyGems, Cargo, NuGet, Go

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2 certified. US data residency. OAuth2 with scopes. Security-first company — cybersecurity veterans. Source code analysis for malware detection requires careful access controls. OpenAPI spec enables client verification.
